programme.sub-title: Backwards Birds and Slimy Sea Creatures

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Puppy Dog Tails and Crocodile Jaws
programme.desc: What a dog is saying when it wags its tail, and the secret behind a crocodiles powerful jaws - and how to escape it

programme.title: Little Giants

programme.title: Little Giants

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers follow a lead to a remote river in Greenland, believed to hold huge Arctic char. They also investigate a myth of Atlantic salmon occupying a lone river

programme.title: Sharks and the City: New York
programme.desc: Biologist Craig OConnell finds out whether the return of seals to the New York City harbour could also turn around the areas declining great white sharks numbers

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Mireya and Ronny make a powerful discovery in the area around the nest. Meanwhile, Bryce suspects Bigfoot is emoting infrasound to repel the team from its territory

programme.title: Lair of the Killer Crocs
programme.desc: The number of crocodiles in Australia has boomed - and they are getting bigger and more deadly. This documentary investigates attempts to relocate them

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The process that caterpillars go through to transform into magnificent butterflies while in their cocoon. Plus, the facts about cats tongues

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: Renowned bear handler Jeff Watson sets out on an adventure to teach his two 700lb grizzly bears, Bob and Screech, how to survive on their own in the wild

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett and Wayde reveal information about the fish that customers and fans ask them about the most, before revealing some unseen moments from the series

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: The members of the Emergency Animal Medical Technician team in Phoenix have their work cut out as they rescue and care for animals in trouble

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toyboy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is sent to speak to the father of the boy bullying his godson - and discovers bad behaviour runs in the family

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a victim of domestic abuse in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a womens refuge. Hettys quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, chaired by Leonard Sachs. Mike Reid, the Beverley Sisters, Valerie Monese, Graham Clark and members of the Players Theatre, London, entertain an audience from the stage of Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Writer, photographer and broadcaster Lucinda Lambton explores the architecture of County Durham, where she grew up fascinated with the landscape, with visits to Durham Cathedral, Beamish Open Air Museum and Lambton Castle

programme.title: Poems in Their Place: WB Yeats
programme.desc: Seamus Heaney presents poems by his fellow Irish poet, visiting Coole Park and Thoor Ballylee Castle in Co Galway. First broadcast in 1982

programme.title: The Life and Loves of Oscar Wilde
programme.desc: A candid portrait of Oscar Wilde and his remarkable family, including revelations by his grandson Merlin Holland and Lady Alice Douglas, great-grandniece of Wildes lover Lord Alfred Bosie Douglas. First aired in 1995

programme.title: The Read
programme.sub-title: The Picture of Dorian Gray: The Read with Luke Thompson
programme.desc: Actor Luke Thompson reads from Oscar Wildes only novel, The Picture of Dorian Gray. A young mans wish that his portrait would bear the marks of time and sin while he remains forever young is mysteriously granted, leading him to live a life of pleasure under the influence of the hedonistic Lord Henry Wotton. But as time goes on, the portrait reveals the consequences of his cruelty and vices

programme.title: The Master Builder
programme.desc: Max Fabers 1958 adaptation of Ibsens play set in a Norwegian home in the late 19th-century. Starring Donald Wolfit and Mai Zetterling

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Writer, photographer and broadcaster Lucinda Lambton explores the architecture of County Durham, where she grew up fascinated with the landscape, with visits to Durham Cathedral, Beamish Open Air Museum and Lambton Castle

programme.title: The Life and Loves of Oscar Wilde
programme.desc: A candid portrait of Oscar Wilde and his remarkable family, including revelations by his grandson Merlin Holland and Lady Alice Douglas, great-grandniece of Wildes lover Lord Alfred Bosie Douglas. First aired in 1995

programme.title: The Read
programme.sub-title: The Picture of Dorian Gray: The Read with Luke Thompson
programme.desc: Actor Luke Thompson reads from Oscar Wildes only novel, The Picture of Dorian Gray. A young mans wish that his portrait would bear the marks of time and sin while he remains forever young is mysteriously granted, leading him to live a life of pleasure under the influence of the hedonistic Lord Henry Wotton. But as time goes on, the portrait reveals the consequences of his cruelty and vices

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetimes experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the story of Ancient Egyptian art through 30 masterpieces. He treks across the Sahara and travels along the Nile to find the rarely seen pieces crafted by the countrys earliest inhabitants, and explores how the works often reflected peoples religion. He is also joined by contemporary artists who are confounded by the technical skills of the craftsmen

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson hosts as team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by guests Sinead Cusak, Ian Ogilvy, Miles Kington and Penelope Keith for a game of word definitions and deceptions. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Robin Ray and Derek Hart. The guest musician is soprano Isobel Baillie. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: The Unseen Alistair Cooke
programme.desc: A profile of the journalist and broadcaster told through interviews with friends and family. The film reveals the man behind the voice of Letter from America and includes previously unseen 8mm footage, shot by Cooke himself, of individuals who sparked his passion for the US, including silent-screen star Charlie Chaplin, Oscar-nominated actress Paulette Goddard and the final interview given by author William F Buckley Jnr

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Rose Wylie: This Rose Is Blooming
programme.desc: Alan Yentob meets the painter, known for her exuberant, large-scale canvases that have been exhibited and sold all over the world. He discovers how her memories and experiences - including her childhood in the Blitz during the Second World War, have helped mould her into the artist she is today, and how she transforms the stuff of everyday experience into new and hitherto unseen painterly visions

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox considers the way art over the past 100 years reflects how nature was swapped for progress in the first half of the 20th century, before rediscovering its beauty in the decades following the Second World War, and how todays artists are reimagining the human races future relationship with nature

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Robin Ray and Derek Hart. The guest musician is soprano Isobel Baillie. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetimes experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the story of Ancient Egyptian art through 30 masterpieces. He treks across the Sahara and travels along the Nile to find the rarely seen pieces crafted by the countrys earliest inhabitants, and explores how the works often reflected peoples religion. He is also joined by contemporary artists who are confounded by the technical skills of the craftsmen

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Chelsea at Hill Dickinson Stadium, and Leeds United v Brentford at Elland Road. A 2-0 victory for the Blues in Decembers reverse fixture brought an end to a four-game winless run Chelsea were embroiled in at the time, while Dominic Calvert-Lewin rescued a point for the Whites in a 1-1 draw when they travelled to Brentford earlier in the campaign. Plus, action from Brighton and Hove Albion v Liverpool, Fulham v Burnley, and Bournemouth v Manchester United

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Nick Hewer, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Scarlett Moffatt, Monty Panesar, Louisa Clein and Shazia Mirza are on their final day in Londonderry, when the delayed seventh participant arrives. Jetting in from the Paralympics in Japan, table tennis star Will Bayley completes the pilgrim band. Eventually they leave Northern Ireland behind them and set sail across the North Channel to western Scotland and join the Kintyre Way. Life on the pilgrimage gets tough as the celebrities are challenged both physically and spiritually, struggling with lack of sleep and difficult questions about faith

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A plot of land in Leeds is a potential goldmine, that is if the new owner can obtain planning permission. Dion Dublin likes whats on offer at a bungalow in Derby, and Tommy Walsh thinks the layout at a south west London flat needs a reconfiguration

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer help two teams look for items of value in Shrewsbury, Shropshire. Christina Trevanion oversees proceedings and also learns the centuries-old skill of clay pipe-making at the Broseley Pipeworks Museum

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher is in London to explore how Christians should approach the topic of money and wealth and meets a businessman who turned his back on earning millions due to his faith

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the last semi-final. Four contenders must face five brutal events before going head to head in the legendary Eliminator for a place in next weeks final. Steel has a score to settle in Hang Tough, Viper charges into Suspension Bridge, and Fire enjoys an epic fight with a firefighter

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: Drama based on JJ Arcanjos series of novels. Pickpocket Gabriel Avery faces a new secret life when he is recruited to Crookhaven, a school for crooks where students are taught to beat criminals at their own game. Starring Lucas Leach, Keith Allen and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: Rivalries intensify during the Break-In Challenge. Meanwhile, Amira deals with the consequences of her brothers betrayal, and Gabriel makes a shocking discovery

programme.sub-title: Comic Relief: More Funny For Money
programme.desc: Jill Scott and La Voix take a look back at the highlights of this years charity extravaganza. Featuring another chance to see the world exclusive of Traitors: The Movie: The Sequel, a special one-off Amandaland sketch featuring lots of famous Amandas, and Dermot OLeary and Alison Hammond finding a creative way to raise money for Comic Relief by robbing a bank

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A look at life after dark on the North York Moors. At the Dark Skies Festival, Margherita Taylor finds out how light pollution is affecting nocturnal wildlife, while Sammi Kinghorn meets the people gathering vital dark-sky data and installing wildlife-friendly lighting to help protect the night. Plus, Adam Henson meets apprentices at a cattle mart meeting

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the ruined Beaumaris Castle on the Island of Anglesey, where valuable finds include a copy of Winnie the Pooh that may be a first edition, a rare ballerina brooch, and a wooden chair featuring carvings by Yorkshire craftsman Robert Mouseman Thompson. Fiiona also discovers why the castle was never completed by King Edward I, and is challenged to a game of Basic, Better, Best by Lisa Lloyd with a collection of unusual toys.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: In the wake of a family tragedy, the Bennet household is thrown into disarray. Mary faces an uncertain future, until her aunt and uncle offer her the chance of a fresh start. At first, Mary struggles to adjust to her new life, but she begins to realise that she can have a life away from the watchful eye of her mother

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary is finding her footing in London, but overhears something which knocks her newfound confidence. Mr Hayward introduces her to poetry, but their budding connection is complicated by Mr Haywards engagement. As Mary wrestles with troubling new feelings, she finds herself caught between duty, a new friendship and the possibility of love

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey searches for one suspect, whilst Frank Napier deals with another. As the evidence mounts and Carey is forced to avert another catastrophe, she begins to fear she might have made a terrible mistake. Piersons first day on the job gets ever more challenging, but as the net tightens for James Whitlock, it looks like SO15 might have made a breakthrough in the case

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Newcastle United v Sunderland at St James Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Nottingham Forest at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Sunderland defeated their rivals 1-0 at Stadium of Light in December in the first Tyne-Wear derby to take place in the league since the 2015/16 campaign. Spurs and Forest are both embroiled in a relegation battle, but the latter were in good form in the reverse fixture, prevailing 3-0. Plus, Aston Villa v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterdays goals

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends matches in the Super League, including Arsenal v West Ham United, Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur, and Manchester United v Everton

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

In the first episode, Clive prescribes a diet of oily fish for a woman on a mission to supercharge her health after shedding weight with the help of weight loss drugs

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: When a 90-year-old RAF veteran sells his antiques to fund nursing care, everyone hopes for an auction day miracle. But a life-size Jesus statue raises eyebrows and a lot of money

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson showcases properties in the Cambridgeshire countryside for a couple of retired scientists returning to the UK from Belgium to live closer to their grandchildren. Jules also stops by the Great Fen Project, where 9,000 acres are being restored to the wetland wonder they once were

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Whitby - A Forbidden Maritime Memoir Resurfaces
programme.desc: In Whitby. Chris Shaw restores a secret journal from Captain Cooks first voyage, while Will Kirk helps an upholsterer to revive a chair belonging to Trevor Baylis, inventor of the wind-up radio and Dominic Chinea feels the pressure when he tries his hand at traditional woodblock printing

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Truemans prepare to welcome a visitor. After Suki dismisses Vinnys olive branch, Eve encourages her to put aside her differences. Bea puts on an overbearing act as Honey arrives home, and she later comes to blows with Linda who questions her motives

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan continues his journey in Kerry, where he joins his brother for a fishing trip on Lough Leane, and fulfills some sporting dreams on the hurling pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: A search for some of the countrys greatest gardens. In the first episode, Diarmuid and Carol re joined by guest judge Prue Leith as they visit Fernhill Garden in Ballynahinch, which is filled with more than 4000 plants, a suburban garden in Jordanstown, and a six acre farmhouse garden near Crumlin, in Co Antrim

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Finals week begins, and the four remaining contenders face the biggest challenge of their careers when they cook for 27 of the UK and Irelands leading chefs at the Goring Hotel. The finalists have four hours to prepare one course each for tonights dinner. They then face a second challenge, to cook a dish that reminds them of home

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Colletta Smith investigates the rising cost-of-living impact for communities as rising prices, wages and higher taxes push businesses and families to the brink. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said that 2026 would be the year that more people would begin to feel positive change, but independent experts say we may have to wait a little longer before we notice a few extra pounds in our pockets

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: Jason and Tyler go to a local pub to watch the Arsenal v Spurs game on TV, while Nikki takes Princess shopping but has a hard lesson to learn. Newly pregnant Karen has her first ultrasound scan

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: Noah is alarmed that Princess and Tylers grandmother has tracked them down, and arranges a meeting at the council office. Jason chases Scott about his investment but is dismayed by his answer

programme.title: Have I Got News for You

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: An incident in which police were staggered by a thief with many disguises who went on to confess far more than they could imagine. Plus, how the net closed in on a gang stealing sentimental jewellery

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janets new boss makes her life a misery, while Gaz resolves to split the lovely Kate from Jonny, who in turn receives an ultimatum to dump his friends or face a return to being single. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith and Will Mellor. First broadacst in 2004

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donnas tutor invites her to have a little extracurricular fun, but she is shocked when it takes place on his sofa. Gaz experiences problems in the bedroom and Janet urges a reluctant Jonny to sample her latest pasty recipe

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna enjoys some filthy brunching with her tutor, while Louise gets Jonny into trouble with the police. Gaz solves his bedroom problem and Janet becomes a vociferous advocate of adventurous sex

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Its Mothers Day in Albert Square, and Lauren and Peter prepare to celebrate with the Beales at No 45. An upset Linda prepares to celebrate the day in the Vic with Elaine as Johnny is still distant with her, but things soon go from bad to worse when shes forced to invite Bea. Jean arrives back in Walford, but its clear there is still friction between her and Zoe over Jasmine

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren gets off to a good start as she successfully sells one of Marks cars. Denise and Kim jump into protection mode when they hear a woman is asking questions about Jasmine. Vinny attempts to tell Suki about Pennys pregnancy, but Suki shuts down the conversation before he can reveal the truth

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Josh and Sandra join Denise and Kim for a drink in the Albert as he searches for answers about Jasmine, but Denise soon puts her foot in it by mentioning Zoe. Lauren and Peter meet a mum whose baby is also visually impaired, to discuss a specialist nursery, but the pair bristle when they realise the cost. Elsewhere, Billy grows concerned about Beas obsession with his family after speaking to Harvey, and Suki comes to blows with Penny and Vinny about the pregnancy

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise and Kim buoy Patrick over Josh and encourage him to write a letter about his life story. The pair head back to Sandras to deliver it, and she encourages Josh to read Patricks letter. Max makes an effort with Linda, and Billys approach causes upset

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The decluttering team land in south London to help single mum Almarie and her 10-year-old daughter Marie overhaul their three-bedroom semi. With Staceys guidance, Almarie sorts through the belongings of her husband Marcus for the first time since his sudden death three years ago, creating space to honour old memories and create new ones. Back at the house Rob builds a stage for budding performer Marie and transforms an outhouse into an art studio

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Femi is feeling the pressure as he tries to make a good impression with Cheddars dad, who gives him a daring proposal - but Pixie proves very distracting. Plus, Michelle hosts a reunion, inviting the eliminated queens to return and reflect on the highs and lows of the series with the finalists

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The sceptical reporter travels to Americas South West to find out why this dustbowl, with little to recommend it to its sparse human population, should be so irresistible to aliens, who have allegedly been visiting for more than 50 years. Along the way he meets a self-styled warrior monk who has declared war on evil extraterrestrials

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Femi is feeling the pressure as he tries to make a good impression with Cheddars dad, who gives him a daring proposal - but Pixie proves very distracting. programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Lorna tries out a sexy new image in a bid to catch Toms attention, while Head of English Grantly Budgen suspects one of his students may be taking drugs and approaches Andrew for help. Chlo takes drastic action to convince everyone she was driving on the night of the crash, and Steph stages a break-in to get rid of the suspicious coursework and avoid losing her job

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: An education inspector pays a visit and brings bad news regarding the schools future. Jack decides the time has come to let Steph go, but she threatens dire reprisals unless he rethinks his actions. Meanwhile, Tom and Izzie fight their feelings for each other, but things come to a head when they find themselves alone

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Reeling from the discovery of a body in the lake, Ember knows it will be only a matter of time before the police find the incriminating voicemails. Learning the truth about who has been abusing Miranda, and the possibility that Mack was responsible for Amys disappearance, Ember is already pushed to breaking point - but then Hannahs safety is threatened

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Ember is forced to confess to her family that human bone fragments were found in the boat hut, but Hannah cant believe her dad was responsible and pleads with Ember to look further. When a new development propels Ember into dangerous territory, the truth is finally revealed, and it proves far more shocking than she ever expected

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan as best friends retreating from reality into a world of movies. However, change is coming to their lives whether they like it or not

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Noa attends a taster day for his new job in Bristol, Evie pulls out all the stops for Film Club - theres a lot riding on tonight. Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie is still reeling from the message she saw on Noas phone. But when the two are left alone together, something extraordinary happens. Is it the start of a new journey for Evie? Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Very average woman Spud cooks, cleans, serves and cares for her father. The only problem is she does it all rather badly - and half-heartedly. A woman who is never seen, unattractive and ignored, she does not feel bad about herself. However, when her father dies, she starts to realise she may have to come out from the shadow and be seen. Comedy drama, written by and starring Siobhán McSweeney

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Reeling from the discovery of a body in the lake, Ember knows it will be only a matter of time before the police find the incriminating voicemails. When a new development propels Ember into dangerous territory, the truth is finally revealed, and it proves far more shocking than she ever expected

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges. Careys assertion that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment gains little traction, so she takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: The bottled prophets are looking unwell and Michael still hasnt asked his questions. Brian is causing havoc at the nursing home and warns Michael and Kacey that time is of the essence, but on their return, all hell has broken out in Marvin Gardens., with the cul-de-sac crammed full of vehicles, strangers and irate neighbours

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Civil servant Paul hosts his hike along the stunning Alnwick coast with a taste of traditional Northumbria

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon for an edition devoted to lambing. From scanning ewes for pregnancy, to the rush of new births, and bottle-feeding vulnerable lambs through their early days, Adam and Anita look at all stages of lambing, following the teamwork, care and expertise that bring new life safely into the world in a county shaped by generations of sheep farming

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Three Morning Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon gets under way with the 60m hurdles, high jump and shot put, and the finals of the womens long jump and mens shot put also take place

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just one positive TB test result could mean quarantine for Lucy and Adams dairy herd and disaster for their move to Scotland. Gayle is banned from the lambing shed to protect her unborn child from an infection transmitted through sheep after birth, which puts extra pressure on Andrew and his dad Terrance. Ally passes on his lambing knowledge to his chidren Bethany and Noah, hoping to increase the numbers of young crofters on Lewis

programme.title: Womens Super League
programme.desc: Leicester City v Aston Villa (Kick-off 2.45pm). All the action from the match at King Power Stadium. The reverse fixture in October saw the teams draw 0-0, but both teams have struggled so far this season and will each be determined to take all three points on this occasion and boost their chances of avoiding relegation

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Three Evening Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon concludes with the long jump and 800m, with Saga Vanninen of Finland the reigning champion after beating Irelands Kate OConnor into second place last year in China. Medals are also awarded in the womens pole vault, 1500m, 800m, 60m hurdles and 4x400m, and the mens 1500m, long jump, 800m and 4x400m

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Walmer, the crew responds to reports of a major incident involving a small boat in the English Channel, on arrival they are faced with mass casualties in freezing water. A 75-year-old skipper and his two young grandsons are aboard a yacht thats lost all power and is drifting towards a wind farm. The north Wales crew must race to rescue the vessel and its passengers before its too late

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: At 1am in Cumbria, a 999 call reports a gang breaking into a supermarket cash machine using high-powered cutting tools. They work quickly, escaping with thousands before police arrive. Its the second ATM targeted in the region within 24 hours. Seventy miles away, news of the raids is shared with West Midlands Police, who are already pursuing a prolific gang linked to similar attacks nationwide. As the task force builds intelligence, Cumbria Police lead the forensic investigation, until a third raid heightens the urgency of the case

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.sub-title: Olesya Zdorovetska, Ronán Ó Snodaigh and Myles OReilly
programme.desc: Three artists set forth on a journey of musical collaboration. Ronán Ó Snodaigh is a world-renowned Irish songwriter, composer, musician, and poet known for his revolutionary bodhrán playing style and being the frontman of Kíla. Myles OReilly is a musician and music-documentary filmmaker with a love for collaboration and experimentation. Joining them is Olesya Zdorovetska, a Ukrainian vocalist, musician, and composer whose work has been described as an emotional and intellectual explosion

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Brìghde Chaimbeul is a leading purveyor of experimental Celtic music and of the Scottish smallpipes. Joining Brìghde on this musical exploration of the meeting points between traditional Irish and Scottish music is Jack Talty, a concertina player, composer and producer from the traditional Irish music hotbed of Co Clare

programme.title: Written by Us
programme.desc: Award-winning concert filmed at Belfasts Grand Opera House celebrating the global impact of songs written by musicians from Northern Ireland. Host Eamonn McCrystal brings together Rachel Tucker, Brian Kennedy, Keith and Kristyn Getty and May McFettridge for an evening of music. From Van Morrison to Snow Patrol and Jimmy Kennedy, the show revisits internationally recognised hits, including Moondance and South of the Border, revealing the Northern Irish writers behind songs that became part of a global soundtrack

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Inside the Rage Machine
programme.desc: The BBCs social media investigations correspondent Marianna Spring uncovers the inside story of how social media algorithms designed to connect people have been helping to tear them apart. Insider testimony and whistleblower documents expose a machine thriving on outrage and division as part of a business model, with radicalisation, real-world violence and fractured societies some of the consequences of a system built to shape how users think, feel and see the world

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best travels along the Foyle river network, criss-crossing his way across the many rivers and streams that make up its great catchment

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, with Health Secretary Wes Streeting; Conservative Shadow Work and Pensions Secretary Helen Whately; President of the Liberal Democrats Josh Babarinde; former leader of the Green Party Caroline Lucas and Reform UKs Head of Policy James Orr facing questions from the audience in Croydon

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: Documentary about the fate of photojournalists John Cantlie and James Foley, who were kidnapped in Syria in 2012. The pair had gone out to the Middle East to chase a scoop about foreign fighters, only for their reporting to draw the attention of extremists - and what began as a tense investigation spiralled into a dangerous encounter

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Spring is in full stride at Longmeadow, and Monty Don gets straight to work, bringing new borders to life, reviving winter-worn houseplants and priming his pelargoniums for a standout summer. Joe Swift enjoys access to the private garden of renowned designer Mary Keen, and at RHS Garden Rosemoor, Carol Klein reveals how fruit and veg can deliver both flavour and flair - no matter the size of your plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Gary from north Wales brings in an electric guitar that once belonged to his father Eddie Clayton - who once played in a skiffle group with none other than a pre-Beatles Ringo Starr. The instrument is now damaged and no longer working, so restorer Julyan Wallis gets to work. Other items are an exquisite bangle worn by the owners grandmother, who left Guyana for the UK in 1962 as part of the Windrush generation, a familys rediscovered set of Kent peg tiles and a leather tallit bag - a Jewish family heirloom passed down through generations when a boy celebrates his bar mitzvah

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Newcastle United v Sunderland at St James Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Nottingham Forest at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Sunderland defeated their rivals 1-0 at Stadium of Light in December in the first Tyne-Wear derby to take place in the league since the 2015/16 campaign. Spurs and Forest are both embroiled in a relegation battle, but the latter were in good form in the reverse fixture, prevailing 3-0. Plus, Aston Villa v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterdays goals

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: The five remaining players from the second heat battle it out for a place in the quarter-finals, facing a puzzle that involves showing their mastery of the most powerful piece on the board, the Queen. They then face off in a challenge that mirrors the final stages of most chess games, when often there is only the King and Pawns left on the board. Presented by Sue Perkins, with analysis from David Howell and Anthony Mathurin

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive visits his favourite tourist spot of the Amalfi Coast, going inland to a mozzarella factory, before hopping across to the island of Capri, where he makes his wife a pair of the local sandals made famous by Jackie Kennedy. He also spends the evening singing folk songs at a nightclub favoured by celebrities including Beyonce and Jay-Z

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show from the gardens of Knebworth House in Hertfordshire. Among the treasures are a Japanese-made satsuma pot, a marine chronometer, a diamond brooch that was almost sold for £1, and a bronze Rolls-Royce emblem that could be worth £10,000 - if it is the genuine article

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine return to test their general knowledge skills in a variety of games across the week

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of Bamburgh in Northumberland

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through old Perthshire. He lends a hand bringing in the cattle and hears how Rob Roy MacGregor might not have been the romantic figure of popular fiction. He also travels along a disused railway to a place where the earth once moved for folk at the Shaky Toun, and enjoys a trek on horseback to Loch Tay, where he goes back to the iron age and learns how to light a fire without matches

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the third semi-final, asking questions on the specialist subjects of Clint Eastwood Westerns, the Dutch painter Hieronymus Bosch, the studio albums of Bruce Springsteen and the footballer Cyrille Regis

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Amol Rajan asks the questions as the quarter-final stage nears its conclusion. Two places in the semi-finals are still up for grabs - and both teams are hoping to take one of them

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: The inside story of how America and China have become locked in a battle for global power and influence since Donald Trump first took office. The documentary begins with Trump inauguration in 2017, when he upended the USs relationship with Beijing. He promised to make China pay - but when he started a trade war, Xi Jinping was ready to fight back

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Hailing from Denmark, Casper Clausen is front man of acclaimed band, Efterklang and is also a solo artist. In this episode he collaborates with Roisín Chambers, a singer whose influences draw on the Connemara lineage of sean-nos singing

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya continues her journey through Spain, where she trains with a team of firefighters and learns how the country is spending billions to fight climate change - money that is coming out of the national defence budget. She then travels to France, witnessing a rocket launch in French Guiana, and learning more about the European Space Agency, which has its base in Paris. In the capital, she also finds out about the countrys generous welfare state, before venturing into the countryside to meet farmers who are fed up with European regulation

programme.title: Ammonite (2020)
programme.desc: In the 1840s, renowned but overlooked fossil hunter Mary Anning lives a near-solitary existence with her mother in Lyme Regis. One day, amateur palaeontologist Roderick Murchison arrives in her shop with his invalid wife, asking to be allowed to learn her hunting techniques. When he departs on an expedition, he leaves Charlotte in Lyme,and asks Mary to keep an eye on his infirm wife. Historical biopic, starring Kate Winslet and Saoirse Ronan

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon for an edition devoted to lambing. From scanning ewes for pregnancy, to the rush of new births, and bottle-feeding vulnerable lambs through their early days, Adam and Anita look at all stages of lambing, following the teamwork, care and expertise that bring new life safely into the world in a county shaped by generations of sheep farming

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: After an intense briefing on the influencer campaign from the Olivias, Freyas misgivings about the whole endeavour are at an all-time high. Jo is in less of a celebratory mood and is stunned to find a surprise visitor at the hen do

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball, Roman Kemp and Clive Myrie show how small, everyday changes can boost wellbeing, from dancing to lift your mood to enjoying mackerel for a healthier heart. In the first episode, Clive prescribes a diet of oily fish for a woman on a mission to supercharge her health after shedding weight with the help of weight loss drugs

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Whitby - A Forbidden Maritime Memoir Resurfaces
programme.desc: In Whitby. Chris Shaw restores a secret journal from Captain Cooks first voyage, while Will Kirk helps an upholsterer to revive a chair belonging to Trevor Baylis, inventor of the wind-up radio and Dominic Chinea feels the pressure when he tries his hand at traditional woodblock printing

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks at some of the best medically themed purchases from the Bargain Hunt archives, including a collection of gruesome slides and a vintage anatomy poster

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The decluttering team land in south London to help single mum Almarie and her 10-year-old daughter Marie overhaul their three-bedroom semi. With Staceys guidance, Almarie sorts through the belongings of her husband Marcus for the first time since his sudden death three years ago, creating space to honour old memories and create new ones. Back at the house Rob builds a stage for budding performer Marie and transforms an outhouse into an art studio

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Perhaps the greatest unexplained mystery of all is what happens after death. Clues to the answer may lie in unusual cases of near-death experience, reincarnation, and how memories can be passed from organ donors to transplant recipients. The veteran actor considers if these phenomena are figments of mans collective imagination or whether they could be real

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at Americas network of national parks, vast, dense expanses of virtually unexplored wilderness. This programme visits the Grand Canyon region, where a strange series of events leads researchers to speculate whether its power comes from forces that cannot be explained

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A plane crash in the Everglades National Park leads to strange hauntings - and some believe the park may exert a dark, transformative power

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The ocean liner Titanic sank on its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York in April 1912. The collision with an iceberg in the North Atlantic led to the loss of more than 1,500 lives. Soon the First World War overshadowed the tragedy, and she almost vanished from memory. Decades later, two very different men had dreams and ambitions to re-discover her

programme.title: The Chase
