programme.desc: In Victoria, a crack team races to build the states VLocity trains of the future. Slippery tracks and kangaroos throw a 5,000-tonne coal train off its path to port

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie ONeill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty)

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Reginalds incorrigible brother Alfie Lynch, a habitual thief, turns up at the nunnery seeking help. He is put up at Mrs Clamss guest house. At an art exhibition in the local gallery, egocentric artist Gerry Ardwell features himself as the main exhibit. The next morning, Ardwell is found dead, bludgeoned by a brass statue, while a valuable painting has been stolen. Alfie Lynch is missing and DI Gillespies main suspect. Sister Reginald pleads with Sister Boniface to find the real culprit, a task made harder when the painting is found in Lynchs room and other suspects have alibis

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship, and visits a therapist for advice on how to resolve the issue. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: Part one of two. The stage adaptation of Janes book endures numerous problems before opening night, including electrical problems, feuding cast members and a notorious arts critic writing a potentially bad review. Meanwhile, Mattie searches for new clues about her missing mother, but an onstage murder brings her to the theatre

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy picks up a lead on a drug dealer operating from a burger van and before long Sun Hills uniform branch are mounting a raid on a skunk factory - where Diane puts herself in great danger by ignoring Callums orders. Back at the station, Phil and Stuart muster as much empathy and sensitivity as they can when CID are called on to sort out a love triangle and find a missing bridegroom in time for his wedding

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Acklands plans for a romantic evening at home are thrown into disarray by the arrival of a shotgun-wielding neighbour

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Proctor provokes Holmes by publicly undermining her, and the two fall out

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: With Carver missing, fears for his safety grow when a youth is arrested in possession of his credit card. Long-running drama with the bobbies and detectives at Londons Sun Hill police station

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Burnside is forced to arrest his colleague Carver on suspicion of murder

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A distant cousin of Freds who has Downs syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his protective mother, but Violet struggles to cope and Shelagh is forced to make other arrangements for his care. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House, and Trixie comes to the aid of a pregnant woman who is in urgent need of dental treatment to avoid an infection. The nurses discover what has happened to Sister Mary Cynthia, a revelation that has distressing consequences for Sister Monica Joan

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the womans younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthias depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith

programme.title: Catherine Cooksons The Gambling Man
programme.desc: Robson Green stars in the title role as Rory Connor, a rent collector who spends his nights at the gaming tables, secure in the knowledge that nobody can turn a card quite like him. Unfortunately, as the stakes get higher, Connor finds himself gambling more than money - with dramatic consequences for himself and those around him. Feature-length drama, with Bernard Hill and Sylvestra Le Touzel

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Two years after crushing Napoleon at Waterloo, the Duke of Wellington sends Army officer Sharpe to India after hearing local Maharaja, Khande Rao, is threatening British interests there. However, it proves to be the rugged soldiers most dangerous mission to date. Sean Bean stars

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Top television spy series Operation QT is filming at the convent when a real bullet is fired instead of a replica, narrowly missing the leading actor. Working with DS Livingstone, Sister Boniface soon works out the real target was the shows producer, Dick Lansky, who has many enemies on and off set and a roving eye for the actresses. Lorna Watson stars

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The murder of Amir Aziz, a prominent anti-fundamentalist Muslim, drags the Lyell team into the murky world of hate-crime and counter-terrorism. Initially thought to be the work of a EDL member whose son was killed by the Taliban, the team begin to suspect the killing is connected to Sarah Begovic, a young woman who left the UK to relocate to Syria, but who recently returned home in secret with her six-month-old son. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Lintern

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad investigates a long-running feud between two family ice-cream businesses, which resulted in a shooting and several armed raids 10 years ago. Standing enlists the aid of his daughter in cracking the case, and she discovers evidence of drug dealing - but Pullman does not approve of her involvement. With Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman, alongside guest star Jodie Kelly

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A distant cousin of Freds who has Downs syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his protective mother, but Violet struggles to cope and Shelagh is forced to make other arrangements for his care. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House, and Trixie comes to the aid of a pregnant woman who is in urgent need of dental treatment to avoid an infection. The nurses discover what has happened to Sister Mary Cynthia, a revelation that has distressing consequences for Sister Monica Joan

programme.title: A Small Light
programme.desc: Miep fights to keep Ottos business running in the hope the Franks will return. After Amsterdams liberation, she searches for Jewish residents of the annexe. Drama, starring Bel Powley

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: Mick and Shazia visit Cornwalls famous Towan Studios where some rare rock memorabilia has been stolen and the owner killed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Quinnans fiancee Jenny is surprised to discover she is part of a greyhound-owning syndicate

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Eva confides in Ronnie that she misses Jimmi after they find common ground discussing a recent criminal case during a radio debate on modern policing. Meanwhile, Julia is concerned that the new campus surgery will not be ready in time for opening after problems with the decorators

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A dead body is found in the new campus surgery just as Julia is making the finishing touches to the grand opening. As Eva leads the police in launching a murder enquiry, Jimmi asks her out for a drink and the other staff members have to fend off journalists wanting to know what has happened

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Amanda Mealing joins the cast as consultant Connie Beauchamp, who immediately gets on the wrong side of Will. Ric spends the day flirting with the new arrival - before she reminds him exactly who is in charge. Owen feels confused over Diane and Mubbs conflicting advice, while Donna comes to the rescue of a drunken and distraught Mickie, who makes a surprising confession

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jay persuades Ruth to take part in a job swap so that his grandmother, who has been admitted, will believe he is a doctor rather than a nurse. The department is then pushed to breaking point by a major incident and it becomes apparent it is likely to fail the assessment, so Jordan takes drastic measures to salvage the situation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Quinnans fiancee Jenny is surprised to discover she is part of a greyhound-owning syndicate

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Its Mitchell v Mitchell at the beauty salon auction, but will the loser find a better way to get even? Phil tries to woo Shirley with mixed results

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley uncovers Phils big surprise and is filled with utter dread, and Ronnie has a huge decision to make. Ian begins to home school Lucy - can he cut it as a teacher?

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen is finally able to get up and about - and see the chaos that awaits her after Tristans party the night before. James puts in an offer for a family home. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Lynda Bellingham and Peter Davison

programme.title: Howards Way
programme.desc: Leo and Abby get ready for the Round the Island yacht race, while Polly reveals she intends to leave Gerald. Maritime drama with the Howard family, starring Maurice Colbourne and Jan Harvey

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu delve into the ultra-competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion with an old Rada friend, presents Sebastian with a choice about his future that could change everything for the agency. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Sharon witness a robbery committed by someone they know, leaving them agonising over whether to turn a blind eye or inform on a mate. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Some Adventures of the Inventor of the Mother Stitch
programme.desc: Barry is shocked to discover his golf captain has moved in next door when Glenda shows a keen interest in her new neighbours. Hobbo tries to help a dejected Morton feel less lonely, while Hobbos driving skills attract unwanted attention when he takes to the wheel of a truck following a visit to an inn

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Mother of All Mistakes - or Is It?
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers the whereabouts of his real mother and decides to pay her a visit, but is unimpressed by the welcome he receives. Marina chains herself to a supermarket trolley in protest against the unreliability of men, while Nelly reveals some information that greatly lifts Hobbos spirits

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Howard Cross the Atlantic Single-Handed?
programme.desc: Hobbo is convinced Nelly is his natural mother and tries to establish where he was born, while Howard vows to row across the Atlantic single-handed in a bid to counter Marinas suggestions that he lacks spontaneity. Barrys clothing problem is solved when he is called to help clear Tobys blocked drain

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A couple attempt to sue their council after developing medical conditions they claim were caused by a local mobile phone mast used to transmit internal police communications. Meanwhile, Deed is approached by the wife of a former Iraqi minister who says her family were poisoned and killed by depleted uranium used in the British Armys shells

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad investigates a long-running feud between two family ice-cream businesses, which resulted in a shooting and several armed raids 10 years ago. programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.sub-title: In Pursuit of the Proper Sinner
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Newly demoted Havers joins Lynley on a double murder investigation. After a girl is killed and her camping companion stabbed, two separate lines of inquiry lead the duo into a murky world of deception, corruption and prostitution in their bid to discover the murderers identity. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small, Timothy West and Simon Williams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel reunites a recovering alcoholic with his estranged wife and daughter, and has a revelation about his own behaviour in the process. Ruth and Michelle bond as they battle to get the new surgerys computer system ready for the opening. Matthew Chambers and Donnaleigh Bailey star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Diane faces a personal crisis that jeopardises her relationship with Owen, when her sister Joanna is rushed to the ward following a car crash. Ric has to deal with the repercussions of his night with Connie, while Will is frustrated by her constant criticism, and Donna gets caught in possession of drugs

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth discovers a devastating secret about her husband Edward, which sends her on a downward spiral. Seeing her at rock bottom, Charlie offers emotional support, but the move comes at a huge cost to himself. Kirstys working day is disrupted by Warrens mind games, Jay and Pollys relationship runs into problems, and Jeff learns whether he still has a job as a paramedic

programme.title: Attenborough and the Mammoth Graveyard
programme.desc: David Attenborough joins a team of archaeologists and palaeontologists excavating a mammoth graveyard discovered by fossil hunters in the prehistoric riverbed of the Thames. The site has also led to the discovery of stone artefacts, providing an insight into the relationship between early humans and mammoths during the Ice Ages. Leading experts in the fields of evolution provide their insight into this unique vision of prehistoric Britain

programme.title: Attenboroughs Wonder of Song
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines seven recordings of animals singing, all documented in his lifetime - including one he recorded himself. The songs are from species as varied as lyrebirds, lemurs and humpback whales, all providing an insight into the way these creatures communicate. The programme also examines new evidence on the purpose of singing, which challenges the long-held belief that it is a mating ritual - it has been theorised that both male and female songbirds sing as part of a survival strategy

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose

programme.title: Attenborough and the Mammoth Graveyard
programme.desc: David Attenborough joins a team of archaeologists and palaeontologists excavating a mammoth graveyard discovered by fossil hunters in the prehistoric riverbed of the Thames. The site has also led to the discovery of stone artefacts, providing an insight into the relationship between early humans and mammoths during the Ice Ages. Leading experts in the fields of evolution provide their insight into this unique vision of prehistoric Britain

programme.title: Attenboroughs Wonder of Song
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines seven recordings of animals singing, all documented in his lifetime - including one he recorded himself. The songs are from species as varied as lyrebirds, lemurs and humpback whales, all providing an insight into the way these creatures communicate. The programme also examines new evidence on the purpose of singing, which challenges the long-held belief that it is a mating ritual - it has been theorised that both male and female songbirds sing as part of a survival strategy

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose

Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Buster Merryfield and John Challis

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A weeping statue in the local church gives Del an idea for a money-making scheme to help a hospice under threat of closure. However, his efforts are thwarted by the weather. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Long-running comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Peter Sallis, Keith Clifford and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.desc: Alex has now been a trainee fertility assistant for two months and meets two couples desperately trying to start their families

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With Ben away, Susan decides to take her mother Grace to a cocktail night at the art gallery, but causes uproar by mistaking a dealer for a courier, and is forced to make amends by taking him out for dinner. Meanwhile, Roger finally pops the question to Abi

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Kenzos father arrives unexpectedly on the Harpers doorstep. Janey decides to grant him access, but soon regrets the decision when he fails to bring his son home. Meanwhile, an unusual request from a couple Susan and Ben met on holiday leads to a startling revelation. Starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: An elderly patient comes to the surgery for treatment and confesses to having an affair with a much younger woman - before dying in the dentists chair. To cause further confusion, the mans wife and mistress come looking for Ben, arousing Susans suspicions. Comedy, guest starring Eric Sykes

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Roger and Abi announce they have set a date for their wedding, which sets Susan thinking about what a disappointment her own big day was. She suggests to Ben they renew their marriage vows, but he is invited to one of the most important football matches of the season - which happens to be on the same day as their ceremony

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben tries to show moral support by driving Michael to his university interview. However, a bit of Dutch courage takes its toll on the teenager, who emerges from the meeting in a dishevelled state. Meanwhile, Susan accompanies her attractive boss on an art-buying trip

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael succeeds in getting the Harpers accepted to appear on The Weakest Link, but some searching questions give the familys dirty washing a good airing on national TV - not least that Susan was once married to a wrestler called Troy the Punisher. John Burgess and Rosemary Leach guest star, with Anne Robinson as herself

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador puppy with an elbow disease, and believes a possible solution might involve fitted plates and stem cell injections. Elsewhere, a one-year-old cat is brought in suffering from a crushed pelvis and a broken tail after a road traffic accident, and a Russian Black Terrier with a cancerous growth is at risk of losing his leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take Thats Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, a 14-year-old boy suffers multiple fractures from a football tackle gone wrong, while in Portsmouth, paramedics meet an 81-year-old woman who has fallen from a stairlift

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ben needs his gall bladder removing as a result of losing 11 stone, while AJ is having an operation to free the painful compressed nerves in her toes

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Connie needs a polyp taking from her womb, Carl is undergoing keyhole surgery to remove torn cartilage from his knee and Brad is having a varicose vein stripped from his leg

programme.title: The Young Midwives of Essex
programme.desc: The young midwives enjoy a night out together. Summah must think about her future on the ward, while Mollie thinks about her future off the ward

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seaths parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Ambers cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF. After a previous miscarriage, Jasmine and Rumen want a second child, while Rumen is in remission for cancer

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Nearing the end of her training, Alex is allowed to assist in complex cases. She meets Holly who has a rare split womb and is attempting her sixth round of IVF with her husband

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Greater Manchester for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williamss condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team create a safe and inspiring home space for the Newcombes, who have a two-year-old son with a very rare lung condition. Trailing breathing tubes, equipment the size of tumble dryers, and no downstairs bathroom have turned their once safe home into a risky space for the family. Nick and the team have amassed a huge group of volunteers for Arlo and the family, as they set to work creating a safe and inspiring space that Arlo and his family can call home

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Carson wants a Nashville pad of her own, but finding the perfect home could be tough

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: The brothers help empty-nesters Karen and Rick, who are looking for a deluxe retreat

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Greater Manchester for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williamss condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Feisty feline Driver has already survived trauma during his seven years of life, when he lost an eye as the result of an accident. His indomitable spirit is about to be severely tested again, as hes rushed into Fitzpatricks as an emergency, suffering from a hip injury and serious trauma having been hit by a car. Seven-year-old Dobermann Dexter has serious spinal problems, collapsing on his back legs and tumbling on his front legs. His owners meet Noel to discuss options for his treatment

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Scans reveal multiple sites of deformity affecting Milo the Dalmatians vertebrae, which are squashing both his spinal cord and the nerves coming out on either side. Noel offers the option of distraction fusion surgery to relieve both the pressure and the pain, while Laura and Max bring in their 10-month-old Chuskie pup Moon, who despite his youth has his life blighted by severe developmental elbow disease

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats nine-month-old cockapoo Betsy, whose owners Sophie and Dan are concerned about a dragging front paw caused by nerve damage, to see if there are surgical options. But when Betsy develops gangrene, she has to have her paw amputated, and in order to salvage the rest of her limb, Noel must replace her amputated foot with a skeletally-anchored implant called a PerFiTS (Percutaneous Fixation to the Skeleton)

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Florida woman Jennifer Mee who, as a teenager, became a media sensation when she suffered a particularly strange ailment - a persistent case of hiccups. But in 2010, she lured a man to a dark alleyway where her friends were waiting to rob him. Instead he ended up being shot dead. Even though she didnt pull the trigger, and was nowhere near when the bullets were fired, Jennifer was sentenced to life without parole. Piers charts the journey through fame and rejection that saw Jennifer descend into a criminal lifestyle, and asks family members and legal experts if they believe her punishment was too harsh

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida, where he hears the story of Ashley Humphrey. As a 20-year-old in 2003, she pumped eight bullets into barmaid Sandee Rozzo - a crime for which she is now serving 25 years. Piers tries to understand how she came to commit the crime, wondering whether she was persuaded by her domineering and abusive husband - who was being accused of rape by the victim - or if she is simply a cold-blooded killer who came up with the plan herself

programme.desc: Theres heartbreak and jubilation as Alex meets two couples desperately trying to defy the odds and become parents

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex has now been a trainee fertility assistant for two months and meets two couples desperately trying to start their families

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex learns about the hormone injections that 3,000 patients a year take at Kings Fertility to prepare for IVF, and meets 34-year-old Sharlotte who is freezing her eggs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With Ben away, Susan decides to take her mother Grace to a cocktail night at the art gallery, but causes uproar by mistaking a dealer for a courier, and is forced to make amends by taking him out for dinner. Meanwhile, Roger finally pops the question to Abi

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Kenzos father arrives unexpectedly on the Harpers doorstep. Janey decides to grant him access, but soon regrets the decision when he fails to bring his son home. Meanwhile, an unusual request from a couple Susan and Ben met on holiday leads to a startling revelation. Starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: An elderly patient comes to the surgery for treatment and confesses to having an affair with a much younger woman - before dying in the dentists chair. To cause further confusion, the mans wife and mistress come looking for Ben, arousing Susans suspicions. Comedy, guest starring Eric Sykes

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Roger and Abi announce they have set a date for their wedding, which sets Susan thinking about what a disappointment her own big day was. She suggests to Ben they renew their marriage vows, but he is invited to one of the most important football matches of the season - which happens to be on the same day as their ceremony

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben tries to show moral support by driving Michael to his university interview. However, a bit of Dutch courage takes its toll on the teenager, who emerges from the meeting in a dishevelled state. Meanwhile, Susan accompanies her attractive boss on an art-buying trip

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael succeeds in getting the Harpers accepted to appear on The Weakest Link, but some searching questions give the familys dirty washing a good airing on national TV - not least that Susan was once married to a wrestler called Troy the Punisher. John Burgess and Rosemary Leach guest star, with Anne Robinson as herself

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador puppy with an elbow disease, and believes a possible solution might involve fitted plates and stem cell injections. Elsewhere, a one-year-old cat is brought in suffering from a crushed pelvis and a broken tail after a road traffic accident, and a Russian Black Terrier with a cancerous growth is at risk of losing his leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take Thats Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, a 14-year-old boy suffers multiple fractures from a football tackle gone wrong, while in Portsmouth, paramedics meet an 81-year-old woman who has fallen from a stairlift

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ben needs his gall bladder removing as a result of losing 11 stone, while AJ is having an operation to free the painful compressed nerves in her toes

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Connie needs a polyp taking from her womb, Carl is undergoing keyhole surgery to remove torn cartilage from his knee and Brad is having a varicose vein stripped from his leg

programme.title: The Young Midwives of Essex
programme.desc: The young midwives enjoy a night out together. Summah must think about her future on the ward, while Mollie thinks about her future off the ward

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seaths parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Ambers cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF. After a previous miscarriage, Jasmine and Rumen want a second child, while Rumen is in remission for cancer

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Nearing the end of her training, Alex is allowed to assist in complex cases. She meets Holly who has a rare split womb and is attempting her sixth round of IVF with her husband

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team create a safe and inspiring home space for the Newcombes, who have a two-year-old son with a very rare lung condition. Trailing breathing tubes, equipment the size of tumble dryers, and no downstairs bathroom have turned their once safe home into a risky space for the family. Nick and the team have amassed a huge group of volunteers for Arlo and the family, as they set to work creating a safe and inspiring space that Arlo and his family can call home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. Enter the DIY SOS builders, who set to work on designer Gabrielle Blackmans vision, although with just nine days to complete the task, theyll need help. Luckily, the local tradespeople are also out in force, giving their time and their skills for free

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Theres an unlikely arrival at the North Yorkshire car auction - an Argentinian Army Mercedes G wagon that saw action in the Falklands War

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has three scooters to collect, while also finding room for a Daimler and a teardrop caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks on his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest priced item at the next auction. But, the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Over the years Derek has learnt to always expect the unexpected. So when Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi drops off his Bedford CA campervan its just another day at the office

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian eras most famous expeditions - Scotts ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, Gwent, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legends home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas, and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor begins the next part of his journey on Mundoo Island, which has provided shelter and a way of life for five generations of one farming family. Martin Clunes joins Colin and Sally Grundy for a day of cattle mustering. He also explores Phillip Island, one of Australias favourite playgrounds, then heads south to King Island, in the blustery Bass Strait between Tasmania and mainland Australia. Martin completes his odyssey with a visit to see one of the regions most iconic creatures, the Tasmanian Devil

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor travels around the coast of the United States to discover what life is like on the surrounding islands. He sets off on a 10,000-mile journey from the west to the east, to explore the vast swathe of islands that are scattered beyond Americas shores. The first port of call on his journey is Hawaii, and his exploration looks beyond its image as a tourist paradise, crammed with sun-kissed beaches and world-class surfing, and into the havoc wreaked by advancing lava fields and smoking craters. Leaving Hawaii, Martin flies 2,500 miles north to the islands of Alaska, where the contrast couldnt be more extreme, from tropical rain forests to snow-fringed glaciers, and from crowded beaches to some of Americas most sparsely populated territories

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer returns with a series that takes him around the world to areas that cannot be fully appreciated without considering the vast influence of their rivers, and hes going to explain how those sacred rivers dont just simply flow through countries - they define them. Simon begins at the source of the worlds longest river, the Nile, and follows it out to sea, taking him from the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and then Egypt, right to the Mediterranean Sea. He also explains how the Nile has forged some of our earliest civilisations and influenced religions. However, the stability of the region looks uncertain, given that populations along its banks are growing, and Nile countries are demanding greater shares of the sacred waters

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler has his mind on making Germany into a great empire by expanding eastwards, but his invasion of Poland tips the world into a violent and bloody war

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The continuing story of the diehards committed to hunt down thousands of Nazi war criminals, some of whom were living in plain sight. One such woman was German Beate Klarsfeld, determined to right the wrongs of her countrys dark past and identify Nazis who had gone unpunished - including the notorious Klaus Barbie. The documentary also tells the story of Josef Mengele - who performed gruesome experiments on Auschwitz inmates - and the son so appalled by his fathers actions he considered turning him in

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh, where Adam Schoon gets a once in a lifetime opportunity to see the Scottish Cup, the most important trophy in Scottish football, and he finds out more about - Andrew Watson, considered the first black international footballer. Items brought in to be valued include a carrier bag full of rare Russian jewels, a collection of dolls house crockery, and a black opal necklace

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Aston Hall in Birmingham, and is joined by poet Benjamin Zephaniah, who grew up nearby and has fond childhood memories of playing in the grounds. Among the items brought in to be valued are some ornate and valuable ceremonial weapons from India, a collection of clocks, a pedal car, and the autographs of the Beatles - given to a fan in return for joining them in a water pistol shoot-out

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Patagonia at the foot of the South American continent, a beautiful and rugged land of mountains, ice and endless wild grasslands, His journey takes him up to the huge South Patagonian ice field, through the foothills of the Andes towards the great Grey Glacier of eastern Chile. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian eras most famous expeditions - Scotts ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, Gwent, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legends home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas, and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor begins the next part of his journey on Mundoo Island, which has provided shelter and a way of life for five generations of one farming family. Martin Clunes joins Colin and Sally Grundy for a day of cattle mustering. He also explores Phillip Island, one of Australias favourite playgrounds, then heads south to King Island, in the blustery Bass Strait between Tasmania and mainland Australia. Martin completes his odyssey with a visit to see one of the regions most iconic creatures, the Tasmanian Devil

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor travels around the coast of the United States to discover what life is like on the surrounding islands. He sets off on a 10,000-mile journey from the west to the east, to explore the vast swathe of islands that are scattered beyond Americas shores. The first port of call on his journey is Hawaii, and his exploration looks beyond its image as a tourist paradise, crammed with sun-kissed beaches and world-class surfing, and into the havoc wreaked by advancing lava fields and smoking craters. Leaving Hawaii, Martin flies 2,500 miles north to the islands of Alaska, where the contrast couldnt be more extreme, from tropical rain forests to snow-fringed glaciers, and from crowded beaches to some of Americas most sparsely populated territories

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Documenting the tensions between East and West, which led to a spying game and an arms race that brought the world to the brink of nuclear war. Featuring archive interviews with KGB spy Oleg Gordievsky, who turned double-agent and began working for the British. Plus, a look at the unpublished letters of Michael Bettaney and interviews with former aides to Margaret Thatcher and Ronald Reagan

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Lala drops a bombshell on Raquel that shakes the foundation of their newfound friendship. Sandoval takes some time away from the bar to play rock star with his cover band

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the second part, he discusses becoming only the second player to reach 200 appearances for Munster and how he dealt with anxiety and mental health throughout his rugby career

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Clare native Vincent is attending the animal hospital with Porsche, his once speedy greyhound that he fears is on her last legs. Meanwhile, making the trip from Wicklow is Kealan Doyle and his pet Kunekune pigs Hank and Joey, who are lined up for a badly needed operation. Sapphire, a Shibu Inu dog is brought to the Vet Hospital after a sudden decline in her health, but her medical needs come at a huge financial cost for owner Bernadette

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A frantic mother and father bring their daughter to the Royal for whooping cough treatment, and Cheriton is shocked to hear the girl hasnt been vaccinated. When the girl dies the worried staff have to cope with an influx of children in need of immunisation. Mr Rose reveals he intends to operate on himself, and Ormerod pays a home visit to Mrs Lyons. Medical drama, guest starring Honor Blackman

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Ormerod rushes to help following a boating accident but realises hes been sent to the wrong location, and when he finally arrives at the disaster scene his efforts to save a mans life prove futile. Meanwhile, PC Ventress brings two men found fighting over a girl to the Royal, where one promptly collapses due to a brain haemorrhage. Weatherill faces a testing time when she helps deliver a baby and nurse Meryl reveals she may be pregnant. Medical drama series, starring Robert Daws, Michelle Hardwick and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headline stories from 2000 to a soundtrack of contemporary music

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2001, the year when two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York. It also saw the foot-and-mouth outbreak and Eamon Dunphy turn his hand to TV presenting

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Headline stories from 2004, when a tsunami hit South East Asia, smoking was banned in public places and Cian OConnor won Olympic gold - only to have it taken off him

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2005

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer reflects on his life inside and outside of the ring, from growing up as a triplet to the night in 1992 when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic gold medal in boxing

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: Lightning has struck an incoming flight. As engineers assess the damage, high winds lead to runway closures and another plane leaks a potentially dangerous liquid

programme.title: Generation Dating
programme.desc: Two strangers with a substantial age gap are brought together, and each is challenged to find a suitable date for the other

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.desc: Two brokers, one house - who can bring home the buyer as Altman and Tracy go head-to-head over the sale of a historic property?

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills
programme.desc: Part three of three. The reunion concludes with a surprise appearance from familiar face Kathy Hilton, but her presence draws mixed reactions

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: After a wild night out, the hungover Parsifal crew put the pieces of the night back together as they prepare for the second charter of the season

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Tom and Tom host a friends-and-family event at Schwartz and Sandys, but when Lisa questions them about the bars status, they come clean about their dire situation

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: America boasts a species of fish reputed to have committed a number of violent attacks on humans. The alligator gar is said to be as vicious as a shark and as large as an alligator. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Texas on a hunt to uncover the truth about the notorious creature

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A patient who set fire to his own shop (guest star Tony Slattery) attempts to avoid police questioning by leaping into an ambulance and driving off towards the pier - unaware Alun and a sick man are in the back of the vehicle. Meanwhile, Dr Makori fears for her relationship with Dr Fox when he tells her he has been offered a job in Biafra. Medical drama, with Andy Wear, Paul Fox and Kananu Kirimi

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The five-time Paralympian recalls her life as an elite swimmer and her journey to gold in Rio 2016

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, bricklayer Alex, support worker Kevin and plasterer Zhane are all hoping to win a date with Shannon, a self-proclaimed modern-day Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, Amanda, Katy and Alex battle it out to impress Georgina with their style choices. With nothing but their chosen outfits to help her decide, who will Georgina choose to take out on a first date?

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cooking contest as Kelly Holmes, Anne Hegerty, Ranj Singh and Maura Higgins take to the kitchen in head-to-head cooking battles as they each attempt to create the perfect Spanish dish and avoid elimination in the Cook Off. There is panic over tapas, an unfortunate meat burn, some dramatic croquettas and a wobble over a paella - will they manage to finish their Spanish dishes before the time runs out?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford host the quiz offering three engaged couples the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.sub-title: A Year on My Planet Earth
programme.desc: The stories of the scientists and conservationists who used pioneering techniques to uncover new behaviour, enabling the film-makers to producer this extraordinary series

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Reports of attacks in a remote part of Ukraine take extreme angler Jeremy Wade on a hunt for an elusive lake monster, and to the site of the worlds worst nuclear accident - Chernobyls reactor number four. Can he reveal the culprit and deliver it to the scientists searching for evidence of mutant species?

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen return with the celebrity cooking contest in which famous faces are mentored by world-class chefs as they compete in culinary cooking battles. in the opening edition, Carol Vorderman, Abbey Clancy, Pasha Kovalev and Harry Pinero compete to deliver stand-out dinner party dishes and progress to the next round

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh welcomes Maud from Belfast, who takes on the Chasers in the hope of a trip on the Orient Express. Back to the Future fan Richy attempts to win enough for his own DeLorean, and Stuart from Dorset is after enough cash for a campervan

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. His investigations take him to Spain, where he discovers first-hand that as a consequence of a science experiment gone wrong, the Wels catfish is growing to man-eating proportions and is capable of everything depicted in medieval records

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: The show returns to Cardiff, where beautician Leah is waiting to be swept off her feet by the style choices of either law graduate Lloyd, judo champion Aron or student Daffyd. With only their outfits to help her decide, which lad will Leah choose to join her for a romantic date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, coffee shop supervisor Holly, singer Millie and care assistant Megan seek out stylish outfits for actor John, in the hope he may select one of them for a date

programme.title: Andrew and Fergie: An Unusual Relationship
programme.desc: Documentary focused on the life of King Charles IIIs troubled younger brother, who has publicly fallen from grace. His former wife is the outspoken Duchess Sarah Ferguson, who apparently cant avoid trouble in the eyes of the media. Together they make up the most controversial duo in the British royal family

programme.title: Coma
programme.desc: Simon takes matters into his own hands, threatening his old boss Jimmy to fast-track his redundancy payment - otherwise he will reveal that Jimmy got rid of him as part of a personal vendetta. Meanwhile, DS Evans follows up a call Simon made the night before the attack, reporting a threatening local youth

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Journey to the Edge of the World
programme.desc: The comedian boards a Russian cruise ship to make an eight-day trip through the Northwest Passage and visit the sites where explorers have lived and died. Stopping off on Beechey Island, where Victorian naval officer John Franklin was stranded for two years, Billy moves on to Gjoa Haven, a settlement on King William Island where Roald Amundsen stayed 50 years after Franklins expedition. He then heads south to Dawson City, the site of a gold rush in the late 19th century

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for holiday favourites, including indulgent French toast served two ways, as well as Lisas Spanish-style stew served with Johns aioli. Plus, seasoned tofu steaks accompanied by a rainbow salad and homemade sweet chilli sauce, and finally peaches and cream pies and a roasted peach daiquiri for dessert

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: It is Boxing Day at Kempton and the stage is set for a Christmas showdown like no other. A sold-out crowd roars with anticipation as the unbeaten Constitution Hill and brilliant Irish mare Lossiemouth prepare to face off in the Christmas Hurdle. The build-up has been intense - as two of the best jockeys in the saddle also go head-to-head, who will triumph?

programme.title: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programme.desc: Alison meets the most unlikely stray to be wandering the London streets in the form of a rare Romanian mioritic shepherd who has a fear of stairs and doorways. She also helps a tiny Bichon control its bark, and there is organised chaos when a van with seven Belgian shepherd cross puppies arrives from the RSPCA

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: The daily reality for rookie nurses - especially in AandE - as they deal with life-or-death situations and everything in between. Twenty-two-year-old Mikey is part of the trauma team tasked with helping a teenage rugby player brought in by ambulance with severe concussion in Carmarthen. In Aberystwyth, 24-year-old nurse is Leah is unphased when a patient arrives having suffered a freak lawnmower accident which led to an amputation. Amid medical dramas on the wards, theres a big moment for healthcare assistant Caitlin who, after years of studying, is on her way to collect her new nurse uniform, and is excited to join her colleagues in her blues

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.sub-title: David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Sugg
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Suggn pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions make their way to Birminghams Hippodrome Theatre, where guest judge Stacey Solomon - who knows a thing or two about auditioning in front of a live audience, after coming third on The X Factor in 2009 - joins the panel alongside KSI, Alesha Dixon and Amanda Holden

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting contestants think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Richard Bacon, Daisy Arwen Campbell, Neil Jones and Gary Wilmot pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Tati and her team don hazmat gear to clean a filthy home overrun by pets. Window cleaners battle it out for employee of the month and Dublin Citys night crew scrub the streets

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Shergar: The Racehorse and the IRA
programme.desc: Set against the backdrop of IRA activity, this documentary investigates the extraordinary 1983 kidnapping of Shergar, a Derby-winning racehorse and global icon

programme.title: Tour de Fred
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix takes to the streets and roads of Cork City, testing his stamina with a cycle challenge up St Patricks Hill, one of the citys most famous climbs. He samples Corks famous stout at Murphys Brewery, trains with a womens GAA team, discovers Corks past as the worlds butter capital, tries ceili dancing and cycles to Blarney Castle

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Comedy game show in which celebrities team up with family members, with the parents choosing which questions their children will answer and gambling on how many they can get right. The first edition features Match of the Day presenter Gabby Logan and her daughter Lois, reality TV star Pete Wicks and his mum Tracey and journalist Krishnan Guru-Murthy and his daughter Jasmine

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: John is still reeling after his discovery that a proxy attack is possibly being planned on UK soil to target a prominent Iranian dissident, but a visit to an informants widow throws up contradictory evidence. John becomes convinced that there is a cover-up that permeates to the highest level. Meanwhile, Johns refusal to accept voluntary redundancy means his relationship with Claire is on thin ice, and a visit from his in-laws only stokes tensions. Thriller, starring Shaun Evans and Romola Garai

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: News at 12:30
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories from the Virgin Media News team, followed by sport and weather

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Sarah, Liz, Raza and Derick pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants - Michael originally from New Zealand, Roger from Bridgnorth, Maddie from Cardiff and Theresa from Warrington - pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Time Is Money
programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. Each player stands before an hourglass-shaped money timer that begins to drain as the questions are asked - and the only way of stopping it is to answer correctly. Once the money is gone, the contestant is out - and only one can make the final to play for the amount they have managed to save

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Time is running out for one foundling who was discovered on a luggage rack in Paddington station nearly 75 years ago, while another woman hopes DNA will solve the mystery of why she was left next to a church in Liverpool at two days old. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin
